mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an open-source PostgreSQL operator designed to manage PostgreSQL databases in Kubernetes environments. It automates the operational lifecycle of PostgreSQL, making it easier to deploy and maintain databases. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and the reliance on Kubernetes-native features for high availability.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install the operator.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for CloudNativePG.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the CloudNativePG operator
steps.description:
This step installs the CloudNativePG operator in the specified namespace, ensuring that the correct version is used.

```bash

kubectl create namespace cloudnative-pg

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.28.1

```

> Creates a namespace for the operator.
> Installs the operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD) that the operator manages.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  patroni:
    postgresql:
      version: '14'
EOF

```

> Creates a PostgreSQL cluster named 'my-postgres' with 3 instances.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  patroni:
    postgresql:
      version: '14'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

kubectl describe cluster my-postgres -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Lists the PostgreSQL clusters in the specified namespace.
> Describes the cluster to check its status and configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the CloudNativePG operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the PostgreSQL clusters managed by the operator, leading to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> Uninstalls the operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudNativePG operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your PostgreSQL data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.28.1

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Backs up the current cluster configurations.
> Upgrades the operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the clusters are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified cluster name does not exist in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster name and ensure it is created in the correct namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to PostgreSQL'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The PostgreSQL instances may not be running or are misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the status of the PostgreSQL pods using 'kubectl get pods -n cloudnative-pg' and check logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n cloudnative-pg'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the PostgreSQL instances.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource requests/limits in the cluster configuration and redeploy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm release may have been deleted or not installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CloudNativePG operator using the Helm install command.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for PostgreSQL 14, added new metrics for monitoring, and enhanced backup capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.27.x, ensure to review the new metrics configuration in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the PostgreSQL cluster listed with the command 'kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg', and it should show the status as 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  patroni:
    postgresql:
      version: '14'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
